archetype_reasoning: Covers 22 distinct geo markets across multiple domains including Trump/US, Iran/ME, Russia/UKR, LatAm, and European elections, suggesting genuine breadth rather than niche repetition. Directionality of 1.0 is technically unusual but may reflect all-YES trading style rather than LP activity. Entry prices span the full contested range (0.06 to 0.96+), with meaningful positions at genuinely uncertain prices alongside some near-certainty entries that introduce YIELD_HARVESTER contamination.
domain_blindspots: LatAm: 0-1 record, insufficient track record but flagged red
Canada/BC elections: multiple recent losses including near-certainty entries at 0.993 and 0.767 on Carney suggesting overconfidence
NYC mayoral: entered Mamdani YES at near-certainty prices (0.996, 0.999) and lost — poor exit discipline on collapsing probability
domain_summary: Genuine edge exists in Trump_US (69.7% over 33 trades) and Iran_ME (81.8% over 11 trades), both with meaningful sample sizes. Outside these domains, recent performance has been significantly negative, particularly on Canadian and NYC markets where near-certainty entries were made on outcomes that failed to materialise.
signal_weight_reasoning: Strong track record in Trump_US and Iran_ME warrants domain-specific signal weight, but a pattern of doubling down on collapsing probabilities (Mamdani 0.999, Carney 0.993, Platner 0.987) and consistent losses across recent non-core markets reduces overall signal reliability. The 1.0 directionality (all-YES) eliminates NO-side calibration signals entirely.
behavioural_flags: All-YES trader (directionality 1.0) — never trades the NO side in resolved markets, limiting signal diversity
Near-certainty averaging down: multiple entries on same market at degrading prices (Mamdani: 0.999, 0.996, 0.883, 0.575, 0.08 — catching falling knife)
Platner YES at 0.987 after earlier correct entries at 0.40 and 0.48 — chased near-certainty premium into a loss
Open positions include both YES and NO on same markets (Cuomo, Pakistan, Trump mineral deal) — possible hedging or LP behaviour in open book
Entry at 0.001 on Trump approval rating market — appears more like noise/lottery than signal
risk_patterns: Sunk-cost averaging: entering same market at progressively higher prices as outcome seems locked in, then losing
Near-certainty harvesting creating large downside exposure when black-swan outcomes occur
Pakistan military strike YES at 0.806 is highly speculative and large — concentration in a low-probability event
35% Canada tariff YES at 0.96 is near-certainty position — small upside, significant loss risk
Recent resolved trades show a notable losing streak (8+ losses in sequence) suggesting possible tilt or domain overreach

open_positions_summary: Current open book suggests a mixed thesis: speculative long-tail YES bets (Hawley 2028 at 0.006, Tucker Carlson at 0.06, China invades Taiwan at 0.15, Iran declares war at 0.045) alongside near-certainty positions (Cuomo non-Democrat run at 0.999, Canada tariff at 0.96, Trump mineral deal at 0.962). The Pakistan strike YES at 0.806 is the largest genuine-risk open position. The simultaneous YES+NO positions on Cuomo, Pakistan, and Trump mineral deal suggest partial hedging. Overall thesis implies a trader comfortable holding speculative geopolitical event risk alongside near-certain premium harvests.
watch_items: Monitor Pakistan strike position — large near-certainty-level directional bet on a volatile geopolitical event
Track whether Carney/Canada losses represent a temporary domain misstep or a broader calibration deterioration
Watch if the all-YES directionality shifts — any NO trades would be a strong signal of new conviction
Monitor Iran_ME new entries closely given the 81.8% domain accuracy — highest-value mirroring signal
Cuomo dual YES+NO position resolution — will reveal whether this is LP behaviour or genuine hedging strategy
summary: This trader shows genuine forecasting capability with strong historical accuracy in Trump_US (69.7%) and Iran_ME (81.8%) domains, supported by a meaningful 43-trade sample across 22 markets. However, a damaging pattern of near-certainty position chasing — most visibly in the Mamdani and Carney trades — has produced a run of recent losses that erodes confidence in cross-domain reliability. The all-YES directionality (1.0) limits the trader's usefulness as a bidirectional signal source and raises questions about calibration on the NO side. Best used as a DOMAIN_ONLY signal in Trump_US and Iran_ME with discount applied elsewhere.
